reasoning: The base rate for any specific individual winning the Nobel Peace Prize is exceptionally low (<1%) given the hundreds of nominees each year. While Greta Thunberg was a bookmaker favorite around 2019–2020 at the height of the youth climate strike movement, she has continuously been passed over. Furthermore, her recent activism has become more politically polarizing (e.g., her involvement in pro-Palestine/Gaza protests), which significantly diminishes her likelihood of achieving the broad consensus typically required by the Norwegian Nobel Committee, especially relative to other pressing geopolitical crises. Prediction markets (like Polymarket) priced her odds at exactly 2% (0.02) in late August 2025, and other forecasting aggregators consistently reflect this same 2% figure. While traditional sportsbooks offered her at +800 (~11%) earlier in the year, this pricing structure reflects well-known longshot bias and significant bookmaker margins rather than true probability. Given the lack of any recent specific catalysts that would make 2025 her year compared to earlier years, I align my forecast closely with the highly efficient prediction market consensus of 2%.
